terms.definition: Mathematical object |ψ⟩ encoding all possible states of a quantum system
Process by which the wave-function reduces to a single outcome upon measurement
Open question in quantum theory about what causes or constitutes wave-function collapse
Simultaneous holding of multiple mutually exclusive quantum states
Christian doctrine of one God in three distinct Persons—Father, Son, Spirit
Postulated fundamental information field decomposable into Father, Son, Spirit components
Mathematically independent; inner product equals zero
Action rule mapping one vector to another in Hilbert space
Hermitian operator representing total energy of a system
Quantum state that yields a definite value when an operator acts on it
Degree to which parts of a system maintain ordered phase relationships
Greek for ‘Word’; theological term for cosmic rational structure identified with Christ
Vector field operator measuring net outflow from a point
Quantum postulate that probability equals squared modulus of amplitude
Physical scale around 10⁻³⁵ m and 10⁻⁴⁴ s where quantum gravity effects dominate
Structure-preserving mapping between two systems
Measure of disorder or unavailable energy in a system
Theological concept of reconciliation between God and creation, here framed as restoration of coherence
Unconditional self-giving love, posited as binding energy of the Trinity
Quantum field theory of the strong nuclear force between quarks and gluons
